entities.name: TELENOMUS REMUS
entities.description: An egg parasitoid of Spodoptera frugiperda, used in augmentative biological control and found in Africa and the Americas.

entities.name: SPODOPTERA FRUGIPERDA
entities.description: A moth pest, also known as fall armyworm, originating from the Americas and affecting cereals in sub-Saharan Africa.

entities.name: AFRICA
entities.description: The continent where Spodoptera frugiperda is a serious pest and Telenomus remus has been identified in at least five countries.

entities.name: CEREALS
entities.description: Agricultural crops in sub-Saharan Africa that are targeted by the pest Spodoptera frugiperda.

entities.name: BIOLOGICAL CONTROL
entities.description: An economically and environmentally safer alternative to synthetic insecticides for managing pests.

entities.name: SYNTHETIC INSECTICIDES
entities.description: Chemical agents used for the management of the fall armyworm pest.

entities.name: AMERICAS
entities.description: The region of origin for Spodoptera frugiperda and the source of Telenomus remus and larval parasitoids.

entities.name: MORPHOLOGICAL OBSERVATIONS
entities.description: A method used to identify the species of emerged parasitoids.

entities.name: MOLECULAR ANALYSES
entities.description: A method used to identify the species of emerged parasitoids.

entities.name: AUGMENTATIVE BIOLOGICAL CONTROL
entities.description: A biological control method involving the release of parasitoids to increase their population for pest management.

entities.name: CLASSICAL BIOLOGICAL CONTROL
entities.description: A biological control method focusing on the importation of larval parasitoids from the Americas.

entities.name: LARVAL PARASITOIDS
entities.description: Organisms that parasitize the larval stage of Spodoptera frugiperda, suggested for importation from the Americas.
